description:
You are the release manager preparing the next deployment.  
Before declaring the documentation “green”, you must perform a very small Markdown
lint that checks for **over-long lines** (anything longer than 80 visible characters).

Current workspace
-----------------
1. Directory **/home/user/docs** already exists and contains only four Markdown files:
   • introduction.md  
   • deploy_guide.md  
   • changelog.md  
   • README.md  
   (No sub-directories; do not scan anything outside /home/user/docs.)

2. A sub-directory called **/home/user/docs/lint** does **not** exist yet and must be
   created by you.

Required end-state
------------------
After you finish, the system must hold a file located at

    /home/user/docs/lint/line_length.log

whose properties are:

A. File exists, is world-readable (mode 0644 or more permissive), and its
   modification time is “now” (i.e., created by your session).

B. Content follows **exactly** this machine-verifiable format:

   • One offending line per logical line in the log.  
   • Each entry follows the pattern  
     <RELATIVE_PATH>:<LINE_NUMBER>:<LINE_CONTENT>  
     where  
       – RELATIVE_PATH is the path relative to /home/user (no “./” prefix).  
       – LINE_NUMBER is the 1-based line number inside the source file.  
       – LINE_CONTENT is reproduced **verbatim**, including any trailing spaces
         and WITHOUT surrounding quotes.  
   • Lines appear in ordinary lexical order of the file names, and inside each
     file, in ascending line number order.  
   • The log is terminated by a single trailing newline (the very last line ends
     with “\n”).  
   • If no lines exceed 80 characters, the file must still be created but be
     completely empty (size 0 bytes).

Operational constraints
-----------------------
• You may use ONE compound shell command of your choice (pipelines,
  operators, subshells, etc.) executed from anywhere.  
• Do **not** modify the original Markdown files themselves.  
• Do **not** remove or rename anything supplied by the scenario.  
• Create the parent directory **/home/user/docs/lint** yourself with ordinary
  user permissions.

Once the task is completed successfully, automated tests will open the
log file and verify its path, permissions, and that its textual content
conforms exactly to the specification above.
truth:
Initial files present before the agent starts:

1. /home/user/docs/introduction.md with 4 lines (UNIX line endings):
------------------------------------------------------------
# Introduction to Our Deployment Process
                                                            
This document describes how to deploy the application.
It covers prerequisites, environment variables, and step-by-step procedures that ensure a smooth rollout to production.
------------------------------------------------------------

2. /home/user/docs/deploy_guide.md with 2 lines:
------------------------------------------------------------
# Deployment Guide
Make sure you have completed all unit tests, integration tests, and performance benchmarks before proceeding to deployment.
------------------------------------------------------------

3. /home/user/docs/changelog.md with 5 lines:
------------------------------------------------------------
# Changelog
## [1.0.1] - 2023-04-21
- Fixed bug in authentication.
- Improved latency.
- Added support for distributed tracing which lets the system gracefully handle and visualize end-to-end request flow, even under high load.
------------------------------------------------------------

4. /home/user/docs/README.md with 1 line:
------------------------------------------------------------
# Project Documentation
------------------------------------------------------------

No directory /home/user/docs/lint exists yet.

After the agent finishes, the **only** correct content for
/home/user/docs/lint/line_length.log is (including the final newline at the
end of the last line):

docs/deploy_guide.md:2:Make sure you have completed all unit tests, integration tests, and performance benchmarks before proceeding to deployment.
docs/introduction.md:4:It covers prerequisites, environment variables, and step-by-step procedures that ensure a smooth rollout to production.
docs/changelog.md:5:- Added support for distributed tracing which lets the system gracefully handle and visualize end-to-end request flow, even under high load.

(three lines total, each ending with “\n”)

File permissions expected: 0644  
Parent directory /home/user/docs/lint permissions may be 0755.

Any deviation—extra lines, missing lines, wrong order, trimmed content, missing
trailing newline, or a different pathname—must fail the automated tests.
